title: ProtocolLab Secure DNS Performance Contracts
purpose: Define a bounded deterministic DNS semantic corpus with strict diagnostic and standards-based interoperability profiles for DoT, DoH over HTTP/2, DoH over HTTP/3, and DoQ, plus isolated recursive-resolver and classic UDP/TCP diagnostic surfaces.
scope: Authoritative comparisons use a fixture-backed local authoritative response. Resolver-role diagnostics use a selected local recursive resolver, a runner-provided local fixture authority as its only upstream, and a cold cache per measured operation. Public recursive resolvers are excluded. Secure-DNS comparisons retain exact transport, role, and TLS-profile lanes; classic DNS over UDP or TCP and truncation-driven TCP retry are diagnostic calibration only.

requirements.title: Shared deterministic DNS semantics
requirements.statement: Secure DNS scenarios MUST declare one deterministic DNS query and response identity including header flags and section counts, message ID policy, query name, type, class, response code, authoritative answer, TTL, canonical normalized wire length, and canonical normalized wire SHA-256.

requirements.title: TLS and DNS over TLS binding
requirements.statement: Secure DNS scenarios MUST require the versioned secure-DNS TLS profile, TLS 1.3 under RFC 9846, exact ALPN, authenticated dns.plab.test identity, fixed cryptographic and certificate shape, no early data, and explicit connection reuse; DoT additionally MUST require TCP port 853, two-octet network-order length framing, pipelined outstanding-query ID uniqueness, response correlation by ID and question, and no timed-out ID reuse before connection close.

requirements.title: DNS over HTTPS HTTP semantics
requirements.statement: DoH scenarios MUST declare HTTPS scheme and authority, POST path, Accept and request Content-Type, response Content-Type and status, unencoded bare DNS request body length and hash, canonical normalized response length and hash, and HTTP no-store response caching for the TTL-zero fixture.

requirements.title: Exact DoH transport binding
requirements.statement: DoH measurements MUST distinguish HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 and reject fallback to another HTTP version.

requirements.title: DNS over QUIC binding
requirements.statement: DoQ scenarios MUST require the RFC 9250 QUIC binding, doq ALPN, two-octet network-order framing, message ID zero, one transaction per client-initiated bidirectional stream, client and server STREAM FIN completion, explicit session reuse, and RFC 9250 cancellation, transaction-error, and protocol-error behavior.

requirements.title: Secure DNS validity gate
requirements.statement: Secure DNS measurements MUST validate exact TLS and transport binding, certificate identity, query identity, transport-specific message ID and framing policy, response question and authoritative semantics, parse-and-canonical-reserialize normalized length and hash, completion, and zero unexpected malformed, retry, failure, or timeout outcomes; raw response equality MUST NOT be required because valid compression can differ.

requirements.title: Cache and authoritative role isolation
requirements.statement: Secure DNS comparisons MUST use the local authoritative plab.test. zone with the AA bit set, recursion desired and recursion available clear, external upstream access prohibited, cache disabled, TTL zero, and matched endpoint, certificate, connection or session reuse, exact transport stack, and execution topology.

requirements.title: Secure DNS metrics and artifacts
requirements.statement: Secure DNS scenarios MUST declare query rate, latency percentiles, connection contribution, effective concurrency or streams, transferred bytes, completed, malformed, retried, failed and timed-out counts, and required DNS wire, transport, TLS negotiation, validation, stdout, stderr, and normalized result artifacts.

requirements.title: Timeout and retry determinism
requirements.statement: Secure DNS profiles MUST declare connection, handshake, query, and drain timeouts and zero automatic retries; each timeout MUST terminate or cancel only the affected operation according to its transport and increment timed-out operations without replacing the original sample.

requirements.title: Bounded semantic breadth
requirements.statement: The canonical DNS fixture corpus MUST include representative A, AAAA, CNAME-chain, NXDOMAIN, NOERROR/NODATA, and large EDNS/DNSSEC-shaped responses without multiplying every semantic case across every secure transport.

requirements.title: DoH GET binding
requirements.statement: A representative DoH GET scenario MUST use the unpadded base64url canonical query in the dns URI-template parameter, send no request body, declare Accept, and retain exact HTTP version, authority, response Content-Type, cache policy, status, and normalized response validation.

requirements.title: Classic DNS diagnostic calibration
requirements.statement: Classic UDP and TCP scenarios MUST remain diagnostic-only, use the same local authoritative fixture corpus and normalized ID policy, distinguish bare datagrams from two-octet TCP framing, and require an identical-question TCP retry only after a UDP response sets TC for a response exceeding the advertised UDP payload.

requirements.title: Standards-based secure DNS interoperability profile
requirements.statement: ProtocolLab MUST preserve the exact v1 secure-DNS TLS profile as a distinct strict diagnostic lane and MUST provide a versioned interoperability profile that retains authenticated dns.plab.test identity, exact transport ALPN, no fallback, no early data, and explicit connection reuse while allowing DoT and DoH2 to negotiate TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 and requiring DoH3 and DoQ to use TLS 1.3; the interoperability profile MUST require negotiated TLS version, ALPN, cipher suite, key-exchange group, signature scheme, leaf-certificate algorithm and digest, platform provenance, and acceleration provenance to be recorded, and MUST NOT compare results across different profile IDs or effective negotiated parameter cohorts.

requirements.title: Isolated recursive resolver role
requirements.statement: Resolver-role secure-DNS scenarios MUST target a selected recursive resolver, set RD in the query, require RA and clear AA in the response, flush the selected resolver cache before every measured operation, prohibit cache reuse and external upstream access, use a runner-provided local fixture authority as the only upstream, record resolver and upstream proof artifacts, remain diagnostic and non-publishable, and remain incomparable with authoritative-server scenarios; this initial resolver-role surface MUST be limited to DoT and DoH2.
